commands.review_summary:
This PR adds `markCronJobActive(candidate.job.id)` during startup catch-up cron replay and adds a focused regression test for active-marker setup and cleanup.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +126. Total +127 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: startup catch-up currently creates a cron task run without marking the job active, while authoritative task reconciliation relies on that marker. I did not run the repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the contributor must supply real behavior proof; the code-only test blockers are narrow but automation cannot satisfy the proof gate for them.

target.title: fix(cron): mark job active during startup catch-up replay
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- When CronService.start() replays missed command jobs through startup catch-up, the markCronJobActive() call was missing, so isCronJobActive(jobId) stayed false while the catch-up run was in flight.
- The task-registry reconcile keys off that marker for runtime:"cron" tasks, so a catch-up run that outlived TASK_RECONCILE_GRACE_MS (5 min) was misclassified as lost and emitted a Background task lost system message while the job was genuinely working.
- The heartbeat re-entrancy guard (HEARTBEAT_SKIP_CRON_IN_PROGRESS, which checks hasActiveCronJobs()) was also not engaged for the catch-up window.

Why does this matter now?

- Every gateway restart that replays a missed command job through startup catch-up is affected.
- A run >5 min surfaces a spurious lost task (false terminal projection in the task UI) while the job is genuinely working.

What is the intended outcome?

- isCronJobActive(jobId) returns true while a startup catch-up command job is in flight.
- The task-registry reconcile sees the active marker and does not mark the running task as lost.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to clearCronJobActive (already called by applyOutcomeToStoredJob).
- No changes to the timer loop paths (runDueJob, executeJob) which already call markCronJobActive.

## Linked context

Closes #91695

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Startup catch-up cron runs never set the active marker, so long command jobs are reconciled as lost while still running.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js v22.11.0, OpenClaw latest main (8d08b90489).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Read src/cron/service/timer.ts and confirmed markCronJobActive(candidate.job.id) is now called after tryCreateCronTaskRun in runStartupCatchupCandidate (line 1234).
  - Read src/cron/active-jobs.ts and confirmed markCronJobActive adds to the process-global activeJobIds Set.
  - Read src/cron/service/timer.ts:658 and confirmed applyOutcomeToStoredJob calls clearCronJobActive(result.jobId), restoring mark/clear symmetry.
  - Ran regression test: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/service.startup-catchup-active-marker.test.ts
- Evidence after fix: Regression test output:
  Tests 2 passed (2)
  marks the job active while a missed command job is replayed on startup
  clears the active marker after a startup catch-up command job errors
- Observed result after fix: isCronJobActive("catchup-command") is true while the command is running and false after it completes.
- What was not tested: Live gateway test with actual startup catch-up scenario.

## Tests and validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/service.startup-catchup-active-marker.test.ts — 2/2 passed
- Existing src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts and src/cron/active-jobs-manual-run.test.ts stay green.

## Risk checklist

- userVisible: No (internal cron state management)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Only adds missing mark call; clear path already exists via applyOutcomeToStoredJob.

## Current review state

- Ready for review after this update is pushed.
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
